data.title: Commit-level source attribution via git-native trailers
data.description:
## Context
When investigating bugs in DSO-produced code, plugin maintainers must manually trace git blame → commit message → ticket ID → ticket comments to understand what skill, agent, and task wrote the code. This multi-system archaeology is slow and error-prone — a function's authorship context is scattered across git history, ticket comments, and review artifacts. By automatically appending git-native trailers to commits produced by DSO workflows, the provenance of every code change becomes queryable via standard git log and git blame without leaving the terminal.

## Success Criteria
1. When attribution.enabled=true, commits produced by /dso:commit include DSO trailers appended via git interpret-trailers. Each trailer key (DSO-Skill, DSO-Agent, DSO-Task, DSO-Story, DSO-Epic, DSO-Model, DSO-Review-Score) is included only when its source value is non-empty and from the current session. Source map: DSO-Skill from active-skill-context; DSO-Agent/DSO-Model from active-agent-context (new file, created by this epic); DSO-Task/DSO-Story/DSO-Epic from active task ID via ticket show traversing parent links upward; DSO-Review-Score from reviewer-findings.json.
2. Attribution is opt-in via attribution.enabled config key in dso-config.conf (default: false).
3. For any commit with attribution.enabled=true and a resolvable skill context, git log --format="%(trailers:key=DSO-Skill,valueonly)" outputs exactly the skill name string.
4. When no trailer keys resolve to a non-empty value, the commit message contains no DSO trailer lines and /dso:commit exits 0.
5. Trailers conform to git trailer block convention: blank line separator, each matches ^DSO-[A-Za-z-]+: .+$, no DSO keys in subject or body. Appended as final message transformation.
6. A test commit produced during the sprint session with attribution.enabled=true contains all resolvable trailers, verified via git log -1 --format=%(trailers).

## Dependencies
Soft interaction with 6d90-93d5 (Conventional Commits enforcement) — both modify /dso:commit. Not a hard sequencing dependency.

## Approach
Modify the commit workflow (COMMIT-WORKFLOW.md) to read the current skill from active-skill-context, active task/story/epic from the ticket system (via ticket show + parent traversal), model and agent from active-agent-context (new file), and review score from reviewer-findings.json. Pipe the final commit message through git interpret-trailers with --trailer flags before passing to git commit. Each trailer included only when its source value is non-empty. When no values resolve, message passes through unchanged.

## Scenario Analysis
- Stale active-skill-context (high): Session-scoped file may persist across sessions, silently attributing to wrong skill
- git interpret-trailers version (high): Enterprise-pinned git versions may lack reliable --trailer flag support
- Trailer value corruption (medium): Ticket titles containing colons or newlines could misparse trailer format
- CI parser interaction (high): Commit-lint/release-please may break on trailer-appended messages
- Portability lint interaction (high): Trailer values must use short names to avoid portability hook blocking
- Config whitespace sensitivity (medium): Spaces around = in dso-config.conf may silently disable attribution
- DSO-Model undefined in main session (high): Direct /dso:commit invocation has no agent dispatch context; trailer must be omitted

## Scope Boundary
MVP slice of e407-d35c (Source attribution). Covers commit trailers only. Parent epic retains: /dso:blame command, bug investigation integration, retro analytics, review context enrichment.

### Planning Intelligence Log
- Web research (Step 2.6): not triggered
  - Bright-line conditions that fired: none
- Scenario analysis (Step 2.75): triggered
  - Scenarios surviving blue team filter: 7
- Practitioner-requested additional cycles: none
